mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is an AI Gateway that allows you to call over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It provides features like cost tracking, load balancing, and logging, making it suitable for applications that require interaction with multiple LLMs. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing multiple API keys and the potential for increased latency due to proxying requests.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the LiteLLM CLI tool to interact with the LiteLLM service.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.82.1

```

> Install the specific version of LiteLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install LiteLLM Proxy Server
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM Proxy Server to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.1/deploy/k8s/litellm-deployment.yaml

```

> This command deploys the LiteLLM Proxy Server.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for the LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.1/deploy/k8s/litellm-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command enables autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> This command sets up monitoring for the deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
This will remove the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that any data stored in the deployment may be lost.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment litellm

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command deletes the LiteLLM deployment.
> This command deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. Drain and cordon nodes if necessary, then perform the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n default

kubectl set image deployment/litellm litellm=registry.k8s.io/litellm:1.82.1

kubectl rollout status deployment/litellm

```

> Check the status of the pods before upgrading.
> This command updates the LiteLLM image to the new version.
> Verify that the deployment has been successfully updated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the image tag is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in the deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: '503 Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM Proxy Server may not be running or is overloaded.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the deployment and scale up if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** API keys may not be set correctly in the environment variables.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct API keys are set in the deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLMs including Bedrock and improved logging features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in 'Running' state when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up and the necessary tools installed.
